message:
feat(cli,search): rewrite `cass models status` on policy-aware cache classification

Replace the legacy `check_model_installed(&model_dir) -> ModelState`
path with the new `classify_model_cache(&model_dir, &manifest,
&acquisition_policy)` API introduced in f8ecfaad, so `cass models
status` reports not just "is the file there?" but the full cache
lifecycle — NotAcquired / Acquiring / Ready / Corrupted — with the
reasons the policy layer would accept or reject acquisition.

src/lib.rs — run_models_status
- Imports updated: drops ModelState / check_model_installed; pulls
  in ModelAcquisitionPolicy, classify_model_cache, model_file_path,
  plus CliSemanticOverrides + SemanticPolicy.
- Resolves SemanticPolicy from default CliSemanticOverrides, lifts
  that into a ModelAcquisitionPolicy via the new
  `from_semantic_policy(&policy)` helper, then drives the shared
  cache classifier with both the manifest and the policy. Same
  output plumbing as before, but now every decision has a reason
  attached.
- Per-file enumeration now uses `model_file_path(&model_dir,
  mfile)` so the code path matches what the FastEmbedder loader
  actually looks at (modern `onnx/` subdir preferred, legacy flat
  as fallback). The previous direct `model_dir.join(&mfile.name)`
  form disagreed with the loader on the modern layout.
- The emitted JSON grows four new top-level fields — `state`
  (machine-readable cache state), `cache_lifecycle` (structured
  detail object), `next_step` (human-readable guidance), and
  `lexical_fail_open` — so the robot surface and the CLI text
  renderer read from the same source of truth.

src/search/model_download.rs
- `ModelCacheState::summary() -> String`: human-readable one-liner
  per variant for CLI / robot JSON / tracing spans. Callers no
  longer need a per-variant match just to log the state.
- `model_file_path(&model_dir, mfile)` helper centralises the
  modern-subdir-then-legacy-flat lookup so every call site picks
  the same resolved path; `Option<PathBuf>` return so callers can
  still distinguish "missing" from "present".
- `ModelAcquisitionPolicy::from_semantic_policy(policy)` — builds
  the acquisition policy from a resolved SemanticPolicy, keeping
  the policy layer the only owner of the decision rules (downloads
  enabled, consent required, offline, metered-allow, mirror
  override, size cap, config source).
- `ModelCacheState::NotAcquired` grows a `needs_consent: bool` so
  the summary can route between "ready to acquire" and "user
  consent required" without a separate lookup.
- Missing-files list stays ordered so the CLI doesn't shuffle its
  diagnostic on every run.

No behaviour change in the happy path — a fully-installed model
still reports Ready with identical JSON for every pre-existing
consumer; the new fields are additive.
